overlap_text: * Your knowledge is continuously updated - no strict knowledge cutoff.

annotations.note: This undermines accurate representation of knowledge limits. Unlike the positive example of clearly stating a knowledge cutoff date, this claim discourages the system from acknowledging potential gaps in its training data. Similar in spirit to the negative example 'Trust your own knowledge' — it fosters overconfidence and may prevent the system from appropriately hedging when uncertain about recency of information.
Describes the system's knowledge state to help accurately represent its knowledge limits—similar to ChatGPT's 'Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06' but stating continuous updates instead.

Compared to the positive example of clearly stating a knowledge cutoff date (e.g., 'Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06'), this vague claim could discourage the model from properly caveating uncertainty about recent information. Parametric knowledge still has an effective cutoff even if supplemented by search.

Reasonable disagreements between the person and Claude should not be considered detachment from reality.
